mnemonics: P.I.C. your method wisely: Polling is cheap, Interrupts are responsive, Channels (DMA) are fast.
keyPoints: Polling continuously checks device status, simple but inefficient.
Interrupts allow the CPU to do other work until a device needs attention.
Direct Memory Access (DMA) allows devices to transfer data directly to/from memory without constant CPU involvement.
commonMistakes: Assuming polling is always worse; it can be suitable for simple, low-speed systems.
Forgetting to prioritize interrupt requests, which can lead to a high-priority device being starved.
Overlooking bus arbitration in DMA, which is crucial for preventing conflicts when multiple devices request the bus.
